principles: PostgreSQL-compatible types with explicit primary keys, foreign keys, uniqueness rules, and indexes.
Content, license metadata, normalized chunks, citations, retrieval traces, and embeddings are separate entities.
Production retrieval is allowed only for published document versions, published chunks, valid licenses, and active citations.
Sensitive mutations are attributable through actor, request, trace, status, and audit metadata.
Soft delete is used for user/application records; immutable evidence and audit records are append-only.

global_conventions.identifier_policy: Externally visible identifiers are UUIDs; natural identifiers are constrained as unique columns only when stable.

User.retention: User identity is retained while required for audit; anonymize display fields on deletion where policy permits.

check_constraints.name: ck_source_licenses_valid_range
check_constraints.expression: valid_until IS NULL OR valid_from IS NULL OR valid_until >= valid_from

check_constraints.name: ck_source_licenses_fail_closed
check_constraints.expression: status NOT IN ('persistent_private','persistent_redistributable') OR storage_permission IN ('allowed','conditional')

check_constraints.name: ck_documents_published_has_version
check_constraints.expression: review_status <> 'published' OR published_version_id IS NOT NULL

Document.versioning: DocumentVersion rows are immutable; Document row points to active published version.

check_constraints.name: ck_document_versions_published_frozen
check_constraints.expression: status <> 'published' OR frozen_at IS NOT NULL

check_constraints.name: ck_document_chunks_page_range
check_constraints.expression: page_start IS NULL OR page_end IS NULL OR page_end >= page_start

invariants: published_document_version_required: document_version_id must reference a DocumentVersion whose status is 'published' before status becomes 'active'.
published_chunk_required: chunk_id must reference a DocumentChunk with is_published = true before status becomes 'active'.
license_embedding_permission_required: active embeddings require SourceLicense.embedding_permission IN ('allowed','conditional') and non-expired license status.
Production RetrievalResult rows must reference a published DocumentVersion and published DocumentChunk.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: Conversation and message text may contain sensitive personal or religious questions.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: Mark body/query fields as sensitive, soft-delete user-facing records, audit access, and avoid default full-body logging.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: Provider secrets or API keys could be exposed through provider configuration.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: Store only secret_ref, never plaintext secret values; audit provider/model changes.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: License-expired or prohibited content could enter production retrieval.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: Separate SourceLicense records, fail-closed license status, and enforce published/valid-license invariants before indexing.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: Embeddings could outlive suspended documents or invalidated chunks.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: EmbeddingRecord has explicit active/invalidated status and references published DocumentVersion and DocumentChunk.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: Audit logs may accidentally store full sensitive content.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: AuditLog stores before/after summaries only and marks source context as metadata, not raw payload.

security_and_privacy_risks.risk: Document state transitions could bypass human review.
security_and_privacy_risks.mitigation: Published Document requires published_version_id, approvals are separate append-only records, and transitions must be server-validated and audited.

migration_risks: TASK-02-02 must convert this design into ordered migrations with deferrable references for Document.published_version_id.
Enums should be reviewed before first production migration because enum changes require careful rollout in PostgreSQL.
pgvector dimension is model-specific; migrations should choose vector dimensions per embedding model or separate embedding tables if needed.
citext and pgcrypto extensions are required for case-insensitive email and UUID generation.
